title: Meta Cuts Thousands of Jobs to Focus on Artificial Intelligence
subtitle: The tech giant is changing its business strategy as it moves away from the Metaverse and toward AI development.

summary.en: Meta has announced a major reorganization that includes cutting 8,000 jobs. The company is shifting its focus toward artificial intelligence projects. Along with the layoffs, Meta is canceling plans to hire 6,000 new workers. Many employees are being moved to new teams dedicated to building AI tools. This change comes as the company faces legal challenges and internal concerns about workplace morale.
summary.zh: Meta 宣布進行大規模組織重整，其中包括裁減八千名員工。該公司正將重心轉向人工智慧專案。除了裁員外，Meta 也取消了招募六千名新員工的計畫。許多員工被調往專門開發人工智慧工具的新團隊。此項變革正值公司面臨法律挑戰與內部員工士氣疑慮之際。

paragraphs.en: Meta, the company that owns Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp, has started a large reorganization. This change will shrink its workforce and shift the company’s focus toward artificial intelligence (AI) — computer systems that can perform tasks that usually require human intelligence. According to NPR, Meta will lay off about 10 percent of its workers, which equals approximately 8,000 people. Company spokesperson Erica Sackin confirmed that these employees have already been notified.
paragraphs.zh: 擁有臉書 (Facebook)、Instagram 與 WhatsApp 的母公司 Meta，已啟動大規模的組織重整。這項變革將縮減人力，並將公司重心轉向人工智慧 (AI)——即能執行通常需要人類智慧才能完成之任務的電腦系統。根據美國國家公共廣播電台 (NPR) 報導，Meta 將裁減約百分之十的員工，相當於八千人左右。公司發言人艾莉卡·薩金 (Erica Sackin) 已證實，這些員工皆已收到通知。

paragraphs.en: In addition to these job cuts, Al Jazeera reports that Meta is canceling plans to hire 6,000 new people. The company is also moving 7,000 current employees into new roles. These workers will join teams that focus on developing AI projects and tools. While the company has not shared specific details about which departments are growing or shrinking, this move represents a major change in how the business operates.
paragraphs.zh: 除了裁員之外，半島電視台 (Al Jazeera) 報導指出，Meta 也取消了招募六千名新進員工的計畫。公司同時將七千名現有員工調往新職位，這些員工將加入專注於開發人工智慧專案與工具的團隊。儘管公司尚未透露哪些部門將擴編或縮減的具體細節，但此舉代表該企業的營運模式已出現重大轉變。

paragraphs.en: This shift is part of a larger trend in the technology industry. Many big tech companies are spending billions of dollars to build data centers and hire experts to win the race in AI development. According to NPR, Meta has been trying to catch up to competitors like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google. To support this goal, Meta has predicted that its spending on equipment and buildings this year will be almost double what it spent last year.
paragraphs.zh: 此項轉變是科技產業大趨勢的一部分。許多大型科技公司正投入數十億美元建置資料中心並聘請專家，以在人工智慧開發競賽中勝出。根據 NPR 報導，Meta 一直試圖追趕 OpenAI、Anthropic 與 Google 等競爭對手。為了達成此目標，Meta 預測今年在設備與建築上的支出，將幾乎是去年的兩倍。

paragraphs.en: However, these changes have caused problems for the company. Al Jazeera noted that employee morale is low. Some workers are unhappy because the company is collecting data from them to train its AI models. There are concerns among staff that they are helping to build the very technology that might eventually replace their own jobs. Furthermore, the company has reduced spending on employees, including cuts to annual pay raises.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，這些變動也為公司帶來了問題。半島電視台指出，員工士氣低落。部分員工對於公司收集他們的資料來訓練人工智慧模型感到不滿。員工之間擔憂，他們正在協助打造的技術，最終可能會取代他們自己的工作。此外，公司也削減了員工福利支出，包括減少年度加薪幅度。

paragraphs.en: This restructuring comes at a difficult time for Meta. The company is moving away from the "Metaverse" — a virtual reality world that CEO Mark Zuckerberg once said was the future of the company. Additionally, Meta is facing legal trouble. NPR reported that the company lost court cases in New Mexico and California regarding claims that its platforms harm the mental health of young people. Meta is also expected to return to court in June to face school districts that claim social media causes addiction and mental health issues among students.
paragraphs.zh: 此次重整對 Meta 而言正值艱難時刻。公司正逐漸遠離執行長馬克·祖克柏 (Mark Zuckerberg) 曾稱之為公司未來的「元宇宙」(Metaverse)——一個虛擬實境世界。此外，Meta 正面臨法律糾紛。NPR 報導稱，該公司在新墨西哥州與加州的訴訟中敗訴，這些訴訟指控其平台損害年輕人的心理健康。Meta 預計將於六月再次出庭，面對多個學區的指控，這些學區主張社群媒體導致學生產生沉迷與心理健康問題。

paragraphs.en: For the employees who are leaving the company, Meta has offered severance pay. Al Jazeera reports that workers in the United States will receive 16 weeks of pay, plus an extra two weeks for every year they have worked at the company. These layoffs are part of a wider wave of job cuts across the tech sector as companies change their priorities to focus on new technologies.
paragraphs.zh: 對於即將離職的員工，Meta 提供了資遣費。半島電視台報導，美國員工將獲得十六週的薪資，並依據在公司服務的年資，每滿一年額外加發兩週薪資。這些裁員行動是科技業廣泛裁員潮的一部分，各家公司正因應新技術而調整優先發展項目。

paragraphs.en: As Meta continues this transition, the company remains under pressure to prove that its massive investment in AI will be successful. While the stock market showed a small increase in trading following the news, the company faces significant challenges both inside and outside its offices. Whether this strategy will help Meta succeed in the long term remains to be seen, but for now, the focus is on a major change in direction.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著 Meta 持續進行轉型，公司仍面臨壓力，必須證明其對人工智慧的鉅額投資將會成功。雖然消息傳出後股市交易出現小幅上揚，但該公司在辦公室內外皆面臨重大挑戰。這項策略是否能幫助 Meta 長期成功仍有待觀察，但就目前而言，重點在於方向上的重大轉變。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To change the focus, direction, or position of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（輪班、轉變），這裡作動詞用。文中：This change will shrink its workforce and shift the company’s focus toward artificial intelligence (AI) — computer systems that can perform tasks that usually require human intelligence.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To reach the same level or standard as someone who is ahead of you.
keyTerms.noteZh: 由動詞 catch 與介詞組成，意思非字面上的「抓住」，而是指追趕進度。文中：According to NPR, Meta has been trying to catch up to competitors like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google.

keyTerms.definitionEn: It is not yet known or certain.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個固定慣用語，字面意思容易誤導，實際是指結果還沒出來。文中：Whether this strategy will help Meta succeed in the long term remains to be seen, but for now, the focus is on a major change in direction.

sources.name: NPR News
sources.title: Meta slashes 8,000 jobs as it pivots towards AI

sources.name: Al Jazeera English
sources.title: Meta cuts 8,000 jobs in sweeping global layoffs

quiz.question: According to the article, how is the severance package for departing employees in the United States calculated?

options.A: A flat payment of 16 weeks of salary for all employees.
options.B: A fixed amount based solely on the number of years spent at the company.
options.C: 16 weeks of pay plus an additional two weeks for each year of service.
options.D: Two weeks of pay for every year worked, capped at 16 weeks total.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第六段，文中明確指出美國員工將獲得 16 週的薪資，再加上每在公司工作一年額外兩週的薪資。選項 A 忽略了年資加給；選項 B 錯誤地認為只有年資是計算基礎；選項 D 則錯誤地將 16 週解釋為上限，與原文敘述不符。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the current internal atmosphere at Meta regarding its AI transition?

options.A: Employees are highly motivated to help the company compete with Google and OpenAI.
options.B: Staff members feel conflicted because their work contributes to potential job displacement.
options.C: The company has successfully improved morale by offering new roles to 7,000 employees.
options.D: Workers are excited about the shift away from the Metaverse toward more profitable AI projects.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第四段，員工士氣低落，且員工擔心自己正在訓練的 AI 模型最終會取代他們自己的工作，這顯示出員工對公司策略的矛盾心理。選項 A 與文中提到的低士氣矛盾；選項 C 雖提到職位調整，但並未改善士氣；選項 D 文章中並未提及員工對此轉換感到興奮。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, the word 'transition' is used to describe Meta's current situation. What does it mean in this context?

options.A: A sudden and unexpected collapse of a business model.
options.B: The process of changing from one state or focus to another.
options.C: A legal dispute between a company and its former employees.
options.D: The act of hiring new experts to replace old staff members.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境下，transition 指的是公司從原先的 Metaverse 轉向 AI 發展的過程。選項 A 過於負面且不符合詞義；選項 C 指的是文中提到的法律訴訟，與轉型無關；選項 D 雖然提到人事變動，但 transition 本身是指「轉變」這個過程，而非單指聘僱行為。

quiz.question: What is the primary focus of this article?

options.A: To explain the legal challenges Meta is facing in New Mexico and California.
options.B: To provide a guide on how to survive layoffs in the technology industry.
options.C: To describe Meta's major organizational restructuring and its shift toward AI.
options.D: To argue that the Metaverse was a failed experiment for Mark Zuckerberg.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。整篇文章的核心在於描述 Meta 如何透過裁員、調整職位與增加 AI 投資來進行組織重組。選項 A 只是文章中提到的其中一個挑戰，並非主旨；選項 B 文章並未提供生存指南；選項 D 雖有提及 Metaverse，但那只是背景資訊，並非全文討論的重點。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
